title: calliandra alba
intro: Calliandra alba, also known as white powderpuff, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a shrub-like plant with showy white flowers that resemble powderpuffs, and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. Calliandra alba can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
